title: Fit an aggregate multinomial logit model by maximum likelihood
summary: Estimate a multinomial logit model from coded-long-format choice data. Reads a CSV with respondent, task, alternative, choice, and attribute columns, then runs maximum-likelihood optimization. Writes the model spec, fit state, and per-iteration metrics to run_dir.
description: Reads a coded long-format choice CSV (the output of `choice-wrangling::build-estimation-long`) and fits an aggregate multinomial logit (MNL) model by maximum likelihood. The model assumes a single population-level coefficient vector — no respondent-level heterogeneity — and is the standard fast baseline against which segmented (`model-lc`) and individual-level (`model-hb-mh`) models are compared. Writes the model spec, the fit state, per-iteration metrics, and a CSV export of the coefficient table with standard errors.
usage:
[["model-lg::fit"]]
input = "<coded_long.csv>"
run_dir = "<dir>"
run_name = "lg_run"
# optional:
test_input = "<holdout.csv>"
iterations = 500
convergence_threshold = 1e-8
gradient_threshold = 1e-8
threads = 1

arguments.name: input
arguments.description: Path to the coded long-format CSV file. Expected layout: one row per (respondent, task, alternative), with a `resp_id` column, a `task` column, a response column, and one numeric column per coded covariate. This is exactly what `choice-wrangling::build-estimation-long` produces.

arguments.name: run_dir
arguments.description: Output directory for results. Created if it does not exist. Receives `spec.json`, `fit.postcard` (the persisted fit state), per-iteration metrics, and an `exports/` subdirectory after `finalize` runs (or automatically at end of fit).

arguments.name: run_name
arguments.description: Short identifier for this run. Non-empty string. Used to name output artifacts and subdirectories so multiple runs under the same run_dir do not collide. Must not contain path separators or '..'.

arguments.name: test_input
arguments.description: Optional holdout CSV for out-of-sample evaluation. Must have the same column layout as `input`. When supplied, the run records the holdout log-likelihood under the fitted coefficients as part of the fit summary. For K-fold cross-validation, use `cv-fit` instead.

arguments.name: headers
arguments.description: Whether the input CSVs have a header row. Default `true`.

arguments.name: iterations
arguments.description: Maximum optimizer iterations. Default `500`. The optimizer is a quasi-Newton method (BFGS / L-BFGS), which on a typical MNL converges in well under 100 iterations — the high default cap is generous insurance for harder problems.

arguments.name: convergence_threshold
arguments.description: Stop when the change in log-likelihood per iteration falls below this. Default `1e-8`. The optimizer stops when *either* this or `gradient_threshold` is met.

arguments.name: gradient_threshold
arguments.description: Stop when the gradient norm falls below this. Default `1e-8`. The optimizer stops when *either* this or `convergence_threshold` is met. Both being satisfied is the strongest convergence signal.

arguments.name: rootlik_denominator
arguments.description: Denominator used when computing the per-observation 'root-likelihood' diagnostic. `sets` divides by the number of choice sets, `rows` by the number of rows in the long-format file, and `weight` by the total weight. Choice of denominator affects only the reported root-likelihood value, not the fit itself.

arguments.name: vcov_ridge
arguments.description: Ridge added to the Hessian diagonal before inversion when computing the variance-covariance matrix and standard errors. Default `0.0`. Raise above zero only when the Hessian is near-singular (e.g., a near-collinear coding scheme that would otherwise crash SE computation). The point estimate is unaffected — only the SE matrix uses the ridge.

arguments.name: random_seed
arguments.description: Random seed. `0` (default) means non-deterministic. The MNL fit itself is deterministic (the optimizer is BFGS-style); the seed only affects the rare case of tied starting points.

arguments.name: threads
arguments.description: Number of threads. Default `1`. The likelihood evaluation parallelises over choice sets; speedup is roughly linear up to a few threads then flattens. For typical MNL fits the single-threaded path is fast enough that thread count rarely matters.

details:
`fit` is the MNL workhorse. The model assumes every respondent draws choices from the same coefficient vector, which is fitted by maximising the multinomial log-likelihood across all (respondent, task) choice sets. The fit ignores all respondent-level heterogeneity, which makes it both very fast and the conventional starting point for choice modelling: it's the baseline that segmented (`model-lc`) and individual-level (`model-hb-mh`) models must beat to justify their extra complexity.

The optimizer is a quasi-Newton method. It converges in tens of iterations on most well-conditioned MNL problems and writes per-iteration metrics so you can verify mid-run that the LL is increasing and the gradient norm is decreasing. The Hessian computed at the final point is used to derive standard errors via inversion; if the coding scheme is near-collinear, raise `vcov_ridge` to keep the inversion well-conditioned.

The persisted output is split into two layers, like the other model plugins. The *raw fit state* (`spec.json`, `fit.postcard`, per-iteration metrics) lives in `run_dir`. The *user-facing summary* (coefficient table with SEs, log-likelihood, AIC, BIC) is produced by `finalize`, which is called automatically at the end of a successful run.
value:
On success, `run_dir` contains:

- `spec.json`, `fit.postcard` and per-iteration metrics describing the optimizer run.
- An `exports/` subdirectory with `summary.json` (coefficients, log-likelihood, AIC, BIC) and `coefficients.csv` (the coefficient table with standard errors) — produced by the auto-`finalize` step.

In protocol mode the plugin emits `event.run.started`, periodic `event.run.progress` events with iteration counts, then `event.run.finished` (or `event.run.failed`) carrying the fit summary.
notes: MNL ignores respondent-level heterogeneity. Use it as a baseline and compare against `model-lc::fit` (segments) and `model-hb-mh::fit` (individual-level) before drawing strong conclusions about preferences.
The fit is deterministic; `random_seed` is reserved for tie-breaking edge cases.
Use `cv-fit` for K-fold cross-validation rather than calling `fit` once per fold.
If SE computation fails on a near-singular Hessian, raise `vcov_ridge` — the point estimate is unaffected.

see_also.description: K-fold cross-validation entry point — runs `fit` per fold against a `choice-cv split` directory.
Re-summarise a completed run without re-fitting.
Produces the coded long-format CSV that this command consumes.
Translate the fitted coefficients back into attribute / level form for reporting.
Latent class fit — segments the population.
Hierarchical Bayes fit — continuous individual-level heterogeneity.

examples.title: MNL fit with a holdout for out-of-sample evaluation
MNL fit with a Hessian ridge for SE stability

fields.name: input
fields.description: Path to the coded-long-format CSV file.

fields.name: run_dir
fields.description: Output directory for results. Created if it does not exist.

fields.name: run_name
fields.description: Short identifier for this run. Non-empty string. Used to name output artifacts and subdirectories so multiple runs under the same run_dir do not collide. Must not contain path separators or '..'.

fields.name: test_input
fields.description: Optional holdout CSV for out-of-sample evaluation.

fields.name: headers
fields.description: Whether the CSV has a header row. Default: true.

fields.name: convergence_threshold
fields.description: Stop when change in log-likelihood per iteration < this. Default: 1e-8.

fields.name: gradient_threshold
fields.description: Stop when gradient norm < this. Default: 1e-8.

fields.name: vcov_ridge
fields.description: Ridge added to Hessian before inversion for SE computation. Default: 0.0.

artifacts.title: Coefficient table
artifacts.description: The key modelling output: aggregate MNL coefficients with standard errors, one row per attribute level. Feed into choice-wrangling::expand-coefficients to produce part-worth utilities for simulation.

artifacts.title: Fit summary
artifacts.description: JSON record of the coefficients, log-likelihood, AIC, BIC, and convergence diagnostics.
